title: Tenant Host Identity Resolution Semantic Contract
description: Draft semantic contract for resolving request host identity to tenant context before authentication callback and session handling. The first implementation slice requires active custom-domain ownership to resolve only to the owning tenant and all inactive, unknown, or other-tenant host cases to fail closed.
aiGuidance: Host identity resolution is a semantic prerequisite for tenant-scoped authentication. Do not treat custom-domain source evidence as live-provider login proof.

lifecycle.description: Host identity follows a classify-then-resolve lifecycle: apex, reserved, invalid, and malformed hosts stay global with no tenant, tenant slug hosts resolve by organisation slug, active custom domains resolve through the domain registry, and inactive, pending, or disabled custom domains remain unresolved. Resolution is a pure per-request derivation feeding the request pipeline.

stateModel.description: State is a host classification kind (apex, tenant_slug, reserved_subdomain, invalid_subdomain, possible_custom_domain, or malformed) plus, when resolved, a tenant context containing organisation id, slug, realm name, and host source. Untrusted classifications carry no tenant context.

permissions.description: Host resolution happens before authenticated user permissions exist; it produces tenant context rather than authorizing user action.

contracts.description: The operation surface is a host-identity lookup plus the implicit Host / forwarded-host request context consumed by the gateway pipeline. It returns the classification and any resolved tenant context.

validation.description: Classification covers port stripping, reserved slugs, malformed hosts, unknown apex subdomains, possible-custom-domain hosts, and forwarded-host precedence. Only verified active domains and known slugs validate to a tenant.

errorModel.description: Untrusted or unresolved hosts return a null tenant context fail-closed, and global routes reject apex subdomains with an explicit detail rather than silently serving global content; provider failure returns a typed degraded result. No host is ever resolved to a fallback tenant.

auditModel.description: No mutation audit applies because resolution is a read-only derivation; failed resolution is surfaced through resolver and pipeline warnings. There is no state change to audit.

readinessModel.description: Readiness is invariant-ready when resolver code and the domain registry are available, and degraded mode disables registry-dependent paths while keeping read-only resolution visible. Runtime proof needs local substrate for live resolution and skips honestly when no seeded organisation exists.

proof.description: Runtime domain-identity matrix and custom-domain resolution proofs validate live local resolver behaviour across all host classes, demonstrated in source lineage via hermetic/runtime substrate validation. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: Host identity resolution is request-context semantics, not a direct UI surface in the first slice.
